thread.title: How Raj Thackeray's party helped Uddhav Sena retain Mumbai in Maharashtra
thread.title_full: How Raj Thackeray's party helped Uddhav Sena retain Mumbai in Maharashtra

author: @indiatoday
title: How Raj Thackeray's party helped Uddhav Sena retain Mumbai in Maharashtra
text:
How Raj Thackeray's party helped Uddhav Sena retain Mumbai in Maharashtra
Raj Thackeray's Maharashtra Navnirman Sena candidates pulled away the votes from Eknath Shinde's Shiv Sena, allowing Uddhav Sena to maintain their stronghold in Mumbai.
In Short
- MNS diverted votes from Eknath Shinde's Sena in 8 key Mumbai seats.
- Amit Thackeray contested Mahim; Uddhav Sena won narrowly.
- BJP led Maharashtra with 132 seats, Shiv Sena (UBT) secured 20.
In spite of putting a dismal show in [Maharashtra's assembly elections](https://www.indiatoday.in/elections/assembly/story/mahayuti-maharashtra-assembly-election-result-bjp-women-voters-modi-factor-maha-vikas-aghadi-decoding-2639050-2024-11-23), Raj Thackeray's Maharashtra Navnirman Sena (MNS) played a key role in shaping the outcome of the polls, paving the way for [Shiv Sena (UBT)](https://www.indiatoday.in/elections/assembly/story/uddhav-thackeray-maharashtra-nda-win-mahayuti-mahavikas-aghadi-eknath-shinde-shiv-sena-bjp-2638928-2024-11-23) to take control of Mumbai. Among the 20 seats won by the Uddhav Sena, 10 were decided by margins smaller than the votes secured by MNS candidates. This contribution proved to be critical in helping Uddhav Sena retain its political relevance, especially in Mumbai, where eight of the 10 closely contested victories were recorded.
In 8 of these seats, the MNS candidates [pulled away the votes from Eknath Shinde's Shiv Sena](https://www.indiatoday.in/elections/assembly/story/maharashtra-assembly-election-2024-smaller-parties-poll-result-mahayuti-mva-2628269-2024-11-14?utm_source=global-search&utm_medium=global-search&utm_campaign=global-search), allowing Uddhav Sena to maintain their stronghold in Mumbai.
SEAT-WISE ANALYSIS
Mumbai Seats
Mahim: [Raj Thackeray's son, Amit Thackeray](https://www.indiatoday.in/elections/assembly/story/maharashtra-results-2024-aaditya-thackeray-worli-amit-thackeray-mahim-2638792-2024-11-23?utm_source=global-search&utm_medium=global-search&utm_campaign=global-search), contested this seat and polled 33,062 votes. The Uddhav Sena candidate, Mahesh Sawant, won the seat with a margin of 1,316 votes, defeating Shiv Sena's Sada Sarvankar.
Worli: [Aaditya Thackeray won against Shiv Sena’s Milind Deora by 8,801 votes](https://www.indiatoday.in/elections/assembly/story/worli-assembly-election-result-2024-live-update-milind-deora-aaditya-thackeray-shiv-sena-mns-maharashtra-2638005-2024-11-22?utm_source=global-search&utm_medium=global-search&utm_campaign=global-search). The MNS candidate Sandeep Deshpande received 19,367 votes.
Vikhroli: The Uddhav Sena candidate won by 15,526 votes, polling 66,093 votes. The MNS candidate received 16,813 votes.
Jogeshwari East: The UBT candidate secured victory by a margin of 1,541 votes, polling 77,044 votes, while the MNS polled 64,239.
Diadochi: The winning margin was 6,182 votes, with the UBT candidate receiving 76,437 votes. The MNS polled 20,309 votes.
Versova: The UBT candidate won by 1,600 votes, polling 65,396 votes. The MNS candidate received 6,752 votes.
Kalina: The winning margin was 5,008 votes, with the UBT polling 59,820 votes and the MNS 6,062 votes.
Vandre East: The UBT candidate won by 11,365 votes, polling 57,708 votes. The MNS polled 16,074 votes.
Other Seats
Wani: The UBT candidate won by 15,560 votes, polling 94,618 votes. The MNS candidate received 21,977 votes.
Guhagar: The winning margin was 2,830 votes, with the UBT polling 71,241 votes and the MNS 6,712 votes.
The[ BJP dominated the race in Maharashtra](https://www.indiatoday.in/elections/assembly/story/maharashtra-assembly-election-result-mahayuti-victory-means-for-national-politica-india-bloc-loss-2638866-2024-11-23), winning 132 of the 149 seats it contested, while the Shiv Sena (Eknath Shinde) totted 57 and Ajit Pawar's NCP 41. In contrast, the Congress was able to secure victory in just 16 seats, the Shiv Sena(UBT) in 20 and the NCP(SP) in 10.
